title: commelina erecta subsp. maritima
intro: Commelina erecta subsp. maritima, also known as Coast Dayflower, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Commelinaceae and is native to the southeastern United States and the Caribbean. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Coast Dayflower include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, Coast Dayflower is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
